evals.prompt: I just started a new Rust project and I want to make sure every commit is formatted correctly before pushing. Can you set up git hooks that run cargo fmt automatically?
evals.expected_output: The skill should install a git pre-commit hook that runs cargo fmt before each commit. The hook should be located at .git/hooks/pre-commit and be executable. Instructions should be provided for running the setup script.

evals.prompt: My team keeps pushing Rust code with formatting inconsistencies. We need something that blocks commits if cargo fmt would make changes. Also, the code should actually build before committing.
evals.expected_output: The skill should set up a pre-commit hook that runs both cargo build and cargo fmt --check. If cargo build fails, the commit should be blocked. If cargo fmt --check fails, it should auto-run cargo fmt and then check again.

evals.prompt: I'm working on a Rust project and I'm tired of CI failing because someone pushed unformatted code. Set up git pre-commit hooks for me so this gets caught locally first.
evals.expected_output: The skill should provide a setup script that installs a pre-commit hook. The hook should run cargo build (to ensure it compiles) and cargo fmt (to ensure proper formatting). The hook should auto-fix formatting issues and only allow commits when both checks pass.
